key_point: A viral social media post claims that 5G technology causes cancer and weakens the immune system.
A recent study claims that using essential oils can cure diabetes.
A recent study claims that using essential oils can cure asthma.
A recent study claims that taking probiotics can cure irritable bowel syndrome (IBS).
A viral social media post claims that drinking lemon water daily can cure cancer.
A recent study claims that taking vitamin C supplements can prevent the common cold.

A viral social media post claims that consuming cinnamon can cure Alzheimer's disease within three months. The post cites a study conducted by the University of California, Berkeley, but does not provide any specific details or references.
A recent viral post claims that eating 5 bananas a day can cure high blood pressure within 30 days.
A social media influencer claims that taking a teaspoon of cinnamon daily can significantly reduce the risk of developing type 2 diabetes by boosting insulin sensitivity.
A viral YouTube video claims that consuming a tablespoon of coconut oil every morning prevents all types of cancer.
A recent post on a popular health blog claims that consuming a tablespoon of flaxseed oil daily can completely eliminate high blood pressure within a month.
A popular online article claims that consuming a daily capsule of omega-3 fish oil can increase lifespan by 10 years, citing a study conducted in Japan that followed participants over 20 years.
A recent YouTube video claims that eating 6 raw garlic cloves every morning can prevent heart disease and reduce cholesterol levels.

A viral social media post claims that eating 2 cloves of raw garlic every morning on an empty stomach can completely eliminate high blood pressure within 30 days, citing a study published by the Harvard Medical School.
A recent viral social media post claims that consuming a teaspoon of cinnamon daily can permanently cure asthma without the need for inhalers or other medication.
A recent article claims that consuming 2 tablespoons of coconut oil daily can lead to a significant reduction in cholesterol levels and completely prevent heart disease.
A viral social media post claims that consuming a specific herbal tea blend made from chamomile, peppermint, and ginger can cure chronic insomnia without the need for any medical intervention.
A recent YouTube video claims that consuming a mixture of honey and cinnamon daily can cure arthritis permanently.

A recent blog post claims that consuming a teaspoon of cinnamon every day for a month can cure chronic back pain caused by spinal stenosis, citing a study published in the Journal of Back and Musculoskeletal Rehabilitation.
A viral social media post claims that consuming black seed oil daily can cure HIV within 6 months, citing a recent study published by the University of Oxford.
